title: The Day Lasts More than a Hundred Years
author: Chingiz Aitmatov

setting.note: The Sarozek: a sun-scoured stretch of Kazakh steppe strung along the single-track railway that crosses Soviet Central Asia. A veteran signalman keeps a lonely junction here, and when an old friend dies he sets out with the burial party across the emptiness toward an ancient clan cemetery, only to find the ground swallowed by the fenced-off launch ranges of the space cosmodrome. Legend, railway and rocket share the same horizon.

palette.note: The burnt clay and rust of the Sarozek: scorched steppe earth and the iron of the railway line that runs through the whole book. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
